entry.title: Interest in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hello,

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to express my strong interest in participating in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative. I believe that this program aligns perfectly with my career goals as I am currently transitioning from real estate development to SAP.

In addition to my background as a business analyst and project manager, I have been learning various HCM (Human Capital Management) modules, including Payroll, Compensation, and Security. While I do not have direct working experience, I am eager to apply the knowledge I’ve gained through my studies to further enhance my capabilities by exploring SAP.

Due to personal limitations, I am seeking a career change that better aligns with my current circumstances, and I see SAP as an exciting and innovative platform that offers great potential for growth. I am enthusiastic about the opportunity to build a career in this field and leverage my existing skills in business analysis, project management, and HCM systems.

Thank you for considering my interest, and I look forward to your guidance in this learning journey.

Best regards,
Rama

entry.title: Re: How to import Test Scripts from the SAP Best Practice content for SAP S/4 HANA Cloud into SAP Cl
entry.content:
Hi Nicolas,
Can you let me know, how I can find the "Best Practice Excel file"?
Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Friends helping friends solve the SAP Crossword Puzzle
entry.content:
I am really glad to hear 😸 

P.S. Looks cool to see all that green

entry.title: Re: Friends helping friends solve the SAP Crossword Puzzle
entry.content:
Someone asked and I replied below. If you are familiar with SAP Build Apps, or Transact-SQL, or SAP HANA permissions

https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-discussions/friends-helping-friends-solve-the-sap-crossword-puzzle/m-p/13878136/highlight/true#M1259

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI event script
entry.content:
Hello,

An Installation Check will allow you to review the detailed logs of the installation process. I suggest performing one and attaching it to a case for BC-FES-INS component of SAP Support.

Also refer to https://me.sap.com/notes/3441381/E regarding some common principles for using Event Scripts.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content:
Hello Guys, 

Meet TARA & DOBBY

#petoberfest
#devtoberfest

entry.title: Put method call on external API ECC6
entry.content: Hi all i am working on an integration in which asset details are coming from external sytem and then i have to create asset using bapi then send the assset code back. I can do a get call on api to get the data . But for updating the asset code back i have make a put call on an api. Can somebody guide me if that is possible in abap if yes which resource to use. Sample code would be helpful

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello,

I hope this message finds you well.  I too would like to refer myself as “People looking to switch their careers mid-life”.

I am writing to express my strong interest in participating in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative. I believe that this program aligns perfectly with my career goals as I am currently transitioning from real estate development to SAP.

In addition to my background as a business analyst and project manager, I have been learning various HCM modules, including Payroll, Compensation, and Security. While I do not have direct working experience, I am eager to apply the skills I’ve gained through my studies to further enhance my capabilities by exploring SAP.

Due to personal limitations, I am seeking a career change that better aligns with my current circumstances, and I see SAP as an exciting and innovative platform that offers great potential for growth. I am enthusiastic about the opportunity to build a career in this field and leverage my existing skills in business analysis, project management, and HR systems.

Thank you for considering my interest, and I look forward to your guidance in this learning journey.

Best regards,
Rama

entry.content:
Kira loves #petoberfest

#devtoberfest

entry.title: Re: how to add a field in ALV of transaction IW47
entry.content: Sometimes breakpoints may be tricky to set for enhancements. Try temporarily with the ABAP statement BREAK-POINT. If you still don't stop, it means that your Enhancement Implementation is not active. Did you activate it?

entry.title: Digital skills initiative
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

  • Hope you all are well, my name is Ahmed Elsokkary, I am passionate about SAP Learning Digital Skills Initiative, I have been working in Linux-plus for 1 year as a project manager using SAP Activate methodology before that I have been working as an SAP consultant for 7 years with more than one SAP partner.
  • I really  love working with SAP.  I want to keep learning and growing in this field for lifetime to have a great career opportunity,
  • I'm eager to take a free certification attempt because I believe it would help me to learn more knowledge in SAP and Career Opportunities. 
  • This is truly an amazing initiative by SAP and a great opportunity for me. I'm positively looking forward to hearing back from you.
  • Throughout my career, I've recognized the immense value of staying up-to-date with digital advancements and expanding my skill set in this fast-paced world. sharpening my technical expertise and staying competitive in the evolving digital landscape. I am confident that this initiative will not only enhance my current capabilities but also empower me to contribute more effectively to the digital transformation efforts within my organization and beyond.
  •                   Thank you for considering my application, and I look forward to the possibility of joining                            this incredible learning journey. Please let me know how I can proceed with the                                    registration process.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content:
Kira Code

#petoberfest

#devtoberfest

entry.title: How you can prepare your own template using SAP UI5 Integration Cards locally to Design,Build & Test
entry.content:
Reference : https://ui5.sap.com/test-resources/sap/ui/integration/demokit/cardExplorer/index.html

Steps to preview SAP UI5 Integration Cards in local (Design, Build & Test)

  • Create an application folder
  • create below files index.html, items.json and manifest.json (For Translation create an i18n)
  • preview your application with index.html (right click open with or if you Visual Studio Code using Live HTML)

index.html

 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
	<head>
		<meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge">
		<meta charset="utf-8">
		<title>Card As Custom Element</title>
		<script src="https://ui5.sap.com/resources/sap-ui-integration.js"
				id="sap-ui-bootstrap"
				data-sap-ui-theme="sap_horizon">
		</script>
		<style>
			.myCard {
				margin: 1rem;
				width: 25rem;
			}
		</style>
	</head>
	<body>
		<ui-integration-card
			badge="New"
			id="cardCustomElement"
			manifest="./manifest.json"
			data-mode="Active"
			base-url="./"
			class="myCard">
		</ui-integration-card>
	</body>
</html>

 

items.json

 

[
	{
		"Name": "Comfort Easy",
		"Description": "32 GB Digital Assistant with high-resolution color screen"
	},
	{
		"Name": "ITelO Vault",
		"Description": "Digital Organizer with State-of-the-Art Storage Encryption"
	},
	{
		"Name": "Notebook Professional 15",
		"Description": "Notebook Professional 15 with 2,80 GHz quad core, 15\" Multitouch LCD, 8 GB DDR3 RAM, 500 GB SSD - DVD-Writer (DVD-R/+R/-RW/-RAM),Windows 8 Pro"
	},
	{
		"Name": "Ergo Screen E-I",
		"Description": "Optimum Hi-Resolution max. 1920 x 1080 @ 85Hz, Dot Pitch: 0.27mm"
	},
	{
		"Name": "Laser Professional Eco",
		"Description": "Print 2400 dpi image quality color documents at speeds of up to 32 ppm (color) or 36 ppm (monochrome), letter/A4. Powerful 500 MHz processor, 512MB of memory"
	}
]

 

manifest.json

 

{
	"_version": "1.15.0",
	"sap.app": {
		"id": "card.explorer.translation.list.card",
		"type": "card",
		"i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
		"title": "Sample for HTML Consumption",
		"subTitle": "Sample for HTML Consumption",
		"applicationVersion": {
			"version": "1.0.0"
		},
		"shortTitle": "A short title for this Card",
		"info": "Additional information about this Card",
		"description": "A long description for this Card",
		"tags": {
			"keywords": [
				"Consumption",
				"Card",
				"Sample"
			]
		}
	},
	"sap.card": {
		"data": {
			"request": {
				"url": "./items.json"
			}
		},
		"type": "List",
		"header": {
			"title": "{{TITLE}}",
			"subTitle": "Translation with model. {i18n>SUBTITLE}",
			"icon": {
				"src": "sap-icon://product"
			}
		},
		"content": {
			"data": {
				"path": "/"
			},
			"item": {
				"title": {
					"value": "{Name}"
				},
				"description": {
					"value": "{Description}"
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

 

 

 

Note : you can create any number of cards in your UI5 Application with multiple Integration Cards

you can also generate the structure for manifest.json using Generative AI (using OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, Mistral... etc)

entry.title: Assistance Needed in Configuring Trust Between Cloud Identity Services and SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am currently configuring trust between Cloud Identity Services and SAP BTP Account. I am following the instructions in this article Click Here  but have encountered an issue at Step 6.

According to the documentation, there should be an option labeled "YOUR CORPORATE IDP" in the Default Identity Provider field at Step 5. However, I am not seeing this option. Could someone please assist?

Well, my screen doesn't have this option -

entry.title: Partial goods issue for a consumption Outbound Delivery in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hello SAP Gurus, 

I am faced with the following situation: 

A Production Order has 2 components A and B.

Staging request is made to move both the components to the PSA. However the staging request for only one component A has been fulfilled. 

When this order is confirmed, system creates a consumption OBD. There is a PPF that tries to post the GI for for the OBD, but, it fails to post the GI as it cannot find the stock of component B in the PSA. 

Now, the business wants to at least be able to post the GI for the component that has been staged (in this example component A). At this point, the OBD should move to the GI status 'partially completed'. Later, when they confirm the staging of component B, they say, they should be able to post the GI again - this time consuming the component B, and moving the overall document to GI status 'completed'. 

In SPRO -> EWM -> GI Process ->  Outbound Delivery -> Production Supply -> 'Post Goods Issue for consumption posting', the fields 'Aut GI CPD' and  'Pst Par GI' are marked for my warehouse. 

I also referred to the SAP Note 2353305 on this issue, but this note also talks about the above configuration, which I already have set. 

Any idea, why the partial GI may not be working? 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: How to connect to Database(On-Prem) via SAP Cloud Connector from SAP BTP
entry.content: Seems like environment variable VCAP_SERVICES doesn't contain clientsecret anymore. Documentation still refers to it though: https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/consuming-connectivity-service#loio313b215066a8400db461b311e01bd99b__section_HttpProxy

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order for Returnable Transport Packaging Goods
entry.content:
Hi Yogendra,

Nice informative blog.

I did tried to run this scenario, but am getting an error at the time of MIGO.

Error: M7226 Update Control of movement type is incorrect ( entry 101 _ _ MB_V)

Tried to configure it using OMJJ, but adding a new combination is not allowed under Update control node.

also tried to maintain record in Sm30 table: V_156SC_VC table. For the same it isn’t allowing to maintaining a new record for the same movement type.

can you provide your input what other configuration I am missing for the same.

Regards,

SD

entry.title: SapJCo3: SNC between Java application with multiple SAP ABAP application servers
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently able to setup a functioning SNC between a Java application (through SAPJCo library) and a SAP/HANA system using these 2 scenarios according to the procedures described on this  SAP Note. On the server where the Java application runs, the PSE and cred_v2 files are located in same folder whose path is the value of the SECUDIR environment variable. However, this architecture does not seem workable when if another SAP system is to be SNC connected to same Java application. As far as I have tested, the cred_v2 file supports storing credentials for talking to only a single SAP system. How then can one SNC connect a single Java application to multiple SAP ABAP systems ?

I have searched online and have not found useful threads. One SAP Community thread mentions a link that offers no useful information.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

Over the past few months, I have observed a growing interest among my colleagues and peers in the industry regarding the new tools and innovations introduced in SAP's offerings. The ability of these solutions to streamline processes and improve the customer experience is evident, and it has sparked an exciting wave of enthusiasm from both current and prospective clients.

In line with this, I have been deeply motivated to enhance my own skill set to better support clients and contribute to the evolving ecosystem(success factors). Acquiring certification would provide me with the opportunity to develop further, ensuring I am equipped to deliver greater value and implement these innovations successfully.

Being part of an ever-evolving industry, I recognize the importance of staying ahead with relevant knowledge and certifications. I would love to be considered for any initiatives that enable me to further my expertise and contribute meaningfully to our shared goals. The chance to pursue this certification would be an exciting and invaluable opportunity for my professional growth.

I sincerely appreciate your time and consideration and hope for the opportunity to take part in the digital skills initiative.

Thank you for your support, and I look forward to hearing from you.

entry.title: Re: Create New Production Connector Error
entry.content:
Hi Rahul,

Thank you for your assistance.

I ran into an issue while activating the shop floor element OPC UA Server. I'm encountering the following error: Manufacturing Fault: MESSAGE: One or more errors occurred. (Endpoint does not support the user identity type provided.) REASON: ServiceResultException SEVERITY: Fatal Stack Trace: at SAP.Manufacturing.UaClient.OpcUaClient.EstablishConnection() at SAP.Manufacturing.Connectivity.Agents.OpcUa.UaAgent.EstablishConnection() at SAP.Manufacturing.Connectivity.AgentBaseCore.EstablishConnection() at SAP.Manufacturing.Connectivity.AgentBaseCore.Start()

Please let me know if you have any insights to troubleshoot this issue.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
As an aspiring professional from an underrepresented group in the technology industry, I am excited to apply for the Digital Skills Initiative scholarship. This opportunity aligns perfectly with my goal of launching a career within the SAP Ecosystem.

Why I'm an Ideal Candidate

  1. Underrepresented Background: As a first-generation college student from a low-income background, I've faced numerous obstacles in accessing technology education and career opportunities. Growing up in a rural area with limited exposure to tech, I've had to actively seek out resources to pursue my passion.
  2. Passion for Technology: My interest in technology was sparked when I first learned about ERPs in a business class. I was fascinated by how SAP solutions integrate various business processes, and I've since been exploring SAP's impact on digital transformation through online courses and webinars.
  3. Career Aspirations: My goal is to become an SAP consultant specializing in sustainability solutions. I aim to help businesses leverage SAP technologies to improve their environmental impact and operational efficiency, combining my passion for tech with my commitment to sustainability.
  4. Commitment to Learning: Despite limited resources, I've demonstrated my dedication to acquiring digital skills by completing several online courses in programming and data analytics. I also actively participate in local tech meetups and have taken on leadership roles in my university's computer science club.
  5. Potential Impact: This scholarship will provide me with the formal training and industry connections I need to overcome the barriers I face as an underrepresented individual in tech. By succeeding in the SAP Ecosystem, I hope to serve as a role model and mentor for others from similar backgrounds, encouraging more diverse participation in the tech industry.

By investing in my development through this initiative, you'll not only be supporting my individual growth but also contributing to a more diverse and inclusive technology industry. I am eager to leverage this opportunity to kickstart my career in the SAP Ecosystem and inspire others from similar backgrounds to pursue tech-related fields.

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to the possibility of being part of this transformative program.

entry.title: Re: MDG and S4/HANA
entry.content: Best practice current SAP MDG implementations replicated MDG-F master data objects to S/4 HANA, data integration to SAP FI-CO RTR Global Ledger migration using LSMW

entry.title: Re: MDG on S4/HANA
entry.content: Best practice current SAP MDG implementations replicated MDG-F master data objects to S/4 HANA. data replication using Web Services, data migration using LSMW (Legacy System Migration Workbench replaced by LTMC

@priteshpatel65

entry.title: Re: Custom Code Migration app - Known Problems & Solutions
entry.content:
Hi @OlgaDolinskaja ,

I am using Custom Code mIgration in my SAP S/4 Hana 1809 on-premise system, why don't I have all the functionalities in the Analysis tab? I attach images including the version of the app that I have.

Grettings,

Diego Izurieta

entry.title: BW to datasphere questions
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I come from a BW background, so please excuse my “old-school” approach!

We are starting a project in SAP Datasphere, and I have a few questions:

1. Can we simulate BEx exit variables (step 1 and step 2) in Datasphere? If so, where would be the best place to implement them – in the Analytical View or Fact View? Or should it be handled elsewhere?
2. We have a requirement similar to BEx with two structures, and another requirement involving custom cell-level logic. How can we manage this in Datasphere? Which layer should we handle this in? This is for cashflow reports that require a high level of customization, which I could typically do quickly in BEx.
3. Is it possible to implement a “rule type” concept in Datasphere? If so, how can this be achieved?

We are using SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) as the reporting tool, and we prefer to use Datasphere as the main design concept. However, if you think any of these requirements are better managed in SAC,please let me know.

Thanks a million

regards

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hi community,

We're Software Engineering graduate now looking to dive into the world of SAP.

Keenly interested in SAP Certified Associate - Data Analyst - SAP Analytics Cloud.

I kindly request a voucher as a token of appreciation for completing the SAP learning journey in "Designing Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud" and "Leveraging SAP Analytics Cloud Functionality for Enterprise Planning."

This voucher would greatly assist me both financially and motivationally, helping to advance my career and ultimately benefiting SAP as well.

🙂

Regards,

ZFiori.

Grettings,

Diego Izurieta

entry.title: Error Message from SAP CC: The Subscriber Account 'taxation module' is not modifiable
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to avoid the below error occurring while BP and contract (Joint distribution) distributing to CI & CC. The log generated from FKKCC_MD_MON.
There was a work around to have "taxation" disable in Event 5703 but I would like to avoid the event modification as well.

Error Message from SAP CC:
The Subscriber Account 0000000251/BRIM cannot be modified because: The Subscriber Account 'taxation module' is not modifiable

How can avoid this error at the BP master data itself. Or do we need to do something in SAP CC ? Please suggest.

Thanks,

Rahul.

entry.title: Re: Issue during Deploy MDK App in the Dev Space
entry.content: @Angelo_Ab There is always one MDK editor version in SAP Business Application Studio. You can find current MDK editor version by navigating to the Extensions, as shown below.

entry.title: Re: Standard Report for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content: Hi Arielgwyn, Please try to find the embedded analytics app in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library by filter the Application Type and Line of Business like below:

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4...
entry.content: If the requirement is to use ECC system as user store for Cloud Identity this is a little bit complicated. You have several options here: (1) If you are still on BTP Neo, there is the option to connect ECC to a NW Java system and Cloud Identity on BTP Neo can use Java UME as corporate user store via Cloud Connector https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/corporate-user-store-neo-environment On NW Java system you can configure UME to use any ABAP system as user store. (2) If you are on BTP Cloud Foundry (as most customers), the only corporate user store supported is Microsoft AD. If you can setup a user replication from ECC to AD that would work too. (3) You can setup ECC system as the source of Identity Provisioning and replicate all ABAP users into Identity Directory of Cloud Identity Service. In this case the authentication will happen entirely on BTP but users can login using their ECC user credentials. (4) You can also setup SAP SSO 3.0 as the on premise SAML identity provider. In this case SAP SSO is deployed on NW Java and leverages Java UME. Like option (1) NW Java UME can be configured to use ABAP system as user store so SAP SSO will be able to authenticate based on ECC user credentials. Once SAP SSO is setup you can configure Cloud Identity Service to proxy SAML authentication to SAP SSO system and cloud users will be authenticated directly on SAP SSO logon page.

entry.title: Re: RF
entry.content: This happens when a user1 tried to grab a Warehouse order and assigned a HU to it but went back (F7) and quit the WO. When a user2 enters this WO and uses same HU he can not proceed with F4 and gets this error message, because the HU is still on resource of user1.

entry.title: Re: RF
entry.content:
See include /SCWM/LRF_PICKINGU04, form LOCATE_MOVE_HUS_TO_RESOURCE, line "IF <huhdr>-rsrc NE ps_resource-rsrc. ", if rsrc are not equal,  we proceed to

*       Collect pick-HUs for move to resource
        APPEND <huhdr> TO lt_huhdr_to_move.

and then we proceed to " PERFORM move_hus_to_resource" and then in this form we have the error message.

entry.title: Clarification on Dynamic Field Addition and Runtime Report Customization in Crystal Reports 13
entry.content:
Hello 

I am currently working on a project using Crystal Reports 13 (CR13) with .NET, and I have encountered some limitations when trying to dynamically modify reports at runtime. In particular, I am looking to replicate certain functionalities that were achievable in Crystal Reports 9 (CR9) with VB6, but I am finding that the same flexibility does not appear to be available in CR13.

In CR9 with VB6, we were able to dynamically add FormulaFields, modify grouping and sorting options, and even insert new objects (such as text fields and field objects) into the report at runtime, all based on the data received. This allowed for highly customizable and dynamic reports. However, in CR13, I am struggling to achieve similar outcomes. Specifically, I would like to know:

  1. Is it possible in CR13 to add FormulaFields dynamically at runtime, or must all formula fields be predefined in the report template?

  2. Can I dynamically add new fields or objects (such as text objects or field objects) to a report at runtime in CR13, as was possible in CR9 using the COM-based API? If not, could you please confirm the limitations in CR13 around runtime modifications to report structures?

  3. Is there any official documentation or guidance available that details the exact limitations and capabilities of runtime report customization in CR13 compared to earlier versions (such as CR9)?

I appreciate your assistance and look forward to your guidance on these questions.

entry.title: Re: MD01 MRP Run should create only one PR for multiple material but same contract
entry.content: Not possible. The next best solution might be to use automatic conversion ME59N with converting PurReqs for the same contract into the same Purchase Order.

entry.title: How can i forward outlook mail to sapuser@sapipaddress(sap user name and sap ip address)?
Re: Is there any way to copy a range of assets into List of assets area as in ABAVN
entry.content: worklist does not work for sub assets. is there any other solution?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano 

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
    • First generation college students
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I am willing to learn more about SAP and to undergo the rigors of studying for SAP Certification. I'm learning C_S4CPR Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and Procurement and I awaiting my certification attempt

Thank you

Darius

entry.title: Required Clarification regarding SAP Rise FUE Access based on Activity
entry.content:
In SAP Rise, users are classified into three primary categories: Advanced, Core, and Self-Service. I require clarification regarding the classification of the following activities to determine under which user category they fall:

  • Sales Invoice Creation (VF01)
  • Purchase Order Creation (ME21N)
  • Payable Creation (MIRO)
  • Payable Parking (MIR7)
  • Customer Invoice Clearing (F-28)
  • Vendor Invoice Clearing (F-53)
  • Cash Journal Posting (FBCJ)
  • G/L Accounting Posting (F-02)
  • Customer Down Payments (F-29)
  • Account Receivable (FB70)
  • Account Payable (FB60)
  • G/L account document (FB50)
  • Asset Creation (AS01)
  • Asset Acquisition (F-9c0)

I know the display access is part of self service mostly, I just need to know the creation and posting activity categories. 

Your guidance in identifying the appropriate user categories for each activity would be appreciated.

Thank you for considering my request.

entry.title: Re: Friends helping friends solve the SAP Crossword Puzzle
entry.content:
@VijayCR 

For 19 Down, this blog might help you: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/build-a-chatbot-on-sap-conversational-ai-which-communicates-with-s-4hana/ba-p/13392453

Also, its meaning is to change an object's class to a different class (though generally the shorter form is used).

Or, you could say, it means to re-select the actors for a play.

Or, you could say, it means to again send out your lure/worm into the water when fishing.

Also, its part of the name of Amazon's DVR product.

entry.title: Re: How to connect to Databricks DB using JDBC and extrac...
entry.content:
Hi Colleagues,
At the moment there is no support of Databricks in Cloud Integrations.
Refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/configure-jdbc-drivers.

It would be possible in Edge Integration Cell as soon as support of generic JDBC drivers will be there, refer to the roadmap: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?range=CURRENT-LAST&PRODUCT=000D3A47875C1EDB98A8A910864AC24B&q=jdbc#Q3%202024;INNO=F9E3E0D9286B1EDF83A7F2BB87BDC38E

 

I did not find any requests on Influence portal as well, so consider creating such request if needed:
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2282

 

Best wishes, Stan.

I suggest that you learn the concept and elements of the Enhancement Framework, because you seem to be confused.

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro
entry.content:
Thanks for your question. I'm not sure I fully understand what you are asking. Which technologies are you referring to? How do you want to get involved?

You can always start with a BTP trial system, https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hcp-create-trial-account.html, and then install SAP Build Apps, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZpQM2B1v2GY

entry.title: Re: how to add a field in ALV of transaction IW47
entry.content:
Conclusion: I did the same code as you, i.e. extending OBJECT_TAB via the enhancement spot, adding the new field to the field catalog (CMOD IWOC0004 exit_saplirep1_001 ZXCSVU09), emptied the ALV field catalog buffer (BALVBUFDEL) because IW47/RIAFRU20 uses the ALV buffer, I ran the program, filled by debug the new field in OBJECT_TAB, and this value was displayed by the ALV of IW47.

Sorry, I can't do more.

NB: RIAUFR20 calls the FM REUSE_ALV_FIELDCATALOG_MERGE with the parameter I_INTERNAL_TABNAME="OBJECT_TAB" to determine the initial field catalog, this FM considers all custom fields appended to the included structures (RIHAFVR) but not the fields appended by the Enhancement Framework, hence you need to do an enhancement to update the field catalog for these fields.

NB: exit_saplirep1_001 documentation says "You can modify the field attributes in the field catalog IT_FIELDCAT using this customer exit. However, we must point out that the table as a whole should not be modified, that is, you should neither add nor delete rows."

entry.title: Re: SAP Build version not the same as tutorials.

I am aware that the new REST API interface does no let me create what used to be called "URL placeholders" or custom fields and then add a value in the Test tab. 

Long story short, I will check what is happening and update the tutorials as needed.

I would also give you the current instructions here but I think there is a bug that prevents seeing the ID field in the Get record flow function. I will get back o this thread ASAP.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
Introduction to Universal Revenue Recognition
entry.content:
Universal Revenue Recognition?

What?

Is this a new solution from SAP for recognizing revenue?

To alleviate any excitement or confusion, it is not entirely new. Instead, it combines the already powerful capabilities of event-based revenue recognition and revenue accounting (also known as contract-based recognition) into a unified revenue recognition capability. As we have numerous revenue recognition customers with diverse needs, the main concept behind universal revenue recognition is to offer a comprehensive solution for all revenue recognition needs in one place.

Let me explain.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we offer a revenue recognition platform that comes with pre-delivered content and best practices (scope items) for various end-to-end scenarios and industries, such as projects, sales, services, and subscriptions.

For those who are familiar with the evolution of S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it's worth noting that we have been supporting different turn-key scenarios with event-based revenue recognition (EBRR) from the very beginning. EBRR is utilized by industries that prioritize controlling and profitability reporting. It facilitates real-time calculation and posting of revenue and cost adjustments for sales orders, projects, service documents, and provider contracts, enabling real-time matching of costs and revenue. EBRR also includes market segment information for each revenue recognition posting and requires no reconciliation efforts between controlling and finance data since the information is stored in the Universal Journal. Therefore, EBRR is not only essential for revenue recognition but also a prerequisite for margin analysis and the evolution of Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA).

On the other hand, Revenue Accounting has historically been centered on IFRS 15 / ASC 606 external financial reporting and compliance requirements. It specifically caters to industries such as High-Tech and Telco, providing an accounting perspective on customer contracts that differs from the operational view, with a sophisticated revenue accounting contract management system. In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, its usage has been restricted to BRIM related scenarios, and as a result, it is positioned as Contract-Based Revenue Recognition (CBRR) rather than Revenue Accounting.

Therefore, our approach is to tailor our solutions to individual use cases in various market segments, rather than applying one complex solution to every scenario. Our goal is to make revenue recognition as simple as possible, while still ensuring it meets all necessary requirements.

We believe this is essential because the needs in sales, project, or subscription scenarios can vary significantly. Professional services, for example, are driven by cost and profitability, where the flexibility in resource-related project billing and the percentage-of-completion (PoC) based on plan data plays a crucial role. On the other hand, for product sales in discrete industries, a strong integration into logistics is necessary, and customers require a correlation between cost and revenue for their revenue reporting. In the high-tech industry, we encounter even greater complexity with IFRS 15 due to contract-based revenue streams and frequent contract changes resulting from upsell or cross-sell scenarios.

Figure 1: Revenue Recognition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

We also understand that streamlining revenue management and adapting to ever-changing business models are key factors for the success of every company.

Surely, companies utilize revenue recognition solutions to enable and automate compliance.

However, revenue recognition is more than just a legal compliance topic. In addition to the major regulatory changes in recent years from ASC 606 and IFRS 15, many companies are also facing radical disruptions in how customers purchase products and consume services. The traditional product business is now combined with adding services, or it is translated into a subscription business model. These independent sales and service transactions are increasingly being combined into agreements with multiple elements, allowing the company to offer holistic solutions. This puts additional focus on requirements around margin and profitability analysis.

How does Universal Revenue Recognition now come into play?

Universal Revenue Recognition combines SAP’s revenue recognition capabilities to offer a comprehensive solution for managing revenue. It aims to integrate the strengths of both accounting and controlling (EBRR) and IFRS15, as well as the contract management and allocation mechanisms of CBRR. This allows customers to choose the level of support for revenue recognition they need through configuration options. The following illustration shows the data flow for universal revenue recognition.

Figure 2: Overview Universal Revenue Recognition

Starting with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, it is now possible to activate Universal Revenue Recognition (URR) for specific stock selling scenarios, service contracts, and fixed-price customer projects. If you choose to implement URR, you will need to consider scope items 3KK and 3VS for Contract-Based Revenue Recognition, in addition to the scope items for your respective integration scenario in Event-Based Revenue Recognition.

The URR functionality is optional and requires activation through expert configuration. This option was chosen to allow for a more controlled roll-out and to gather feedback. Once this initial step is completed, the solution can be configured according to your specific needs.

Revenue recognition rules are defined by EBRR revenue recognition keys, which outline the method for recognizing revenue and its posting rules. The key is derived from the item category and material from the operational document, such as a sales order or service contract. When using URR, you must flag the materials and item categories that participate in the URR process during configuration. This standard configuration is also used in the stand-alone EBRR scenario. If you utilize the URR process, you will also need to add relevant configuration for revenue accounting contract management.

Once that is completed, let's briefly review the process shown in figure 2. More detailed information will be provided in subsequent blogs for the specific scenarios.

Let's begin with a sales order in the Sales and Distribution module. In the sales order, you have entered one or more items which, based on previous configuration, are activated for Universal Revenue Recognition. A corresponding revenue recognition key has been configured and, as you are aware from the EBRR integration, derived from the configuration and stored in the sales order item. Additionally, for the sales order integration, you have defined a standalone selling price (SSP) in the SD pricing configuration through condition type PSAM (Step 1).

If the integration with URR has been properly set up, the integration component is triggered and creates a revenue contract and performance obligations. If you have experience with Revenue Accounting, this step is conducted through Inbound Processing – in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we use the Optimized Inbound Processing. For the revenue contract, the revenue is allocated according to the SSP (Step 2).

Subsequently, the follow-on processes are initiated. For a sales order, this involves the delivery and goods issue, with the cost posting. From the cost posting, revenue is recognized in real-time, similar to EBRR, with the exception that the revenue to be recognized is taken from the allocated revenue in the associated revenue contract. Partial deliveries are also supported. Following EBRR posting rules, revenue is debited to an accrued revenue account against revenue adjustment. In the sales order process, the next step is billing for the delivery. Once again, using the logic from EBRR, the billed revenue is deferred in real-time, debiting the revenue adjustment account against deferred revenue (Step 3).

Revenue recognition is event-driven wherever events are available. However, there is also the regular period-end close. In the case of the sales order integration this triggers the balancing of the balance sheet accounts. For other scenarios, where revenue is recognized over time, revenue is recognized as earned for the financial period. This is the case for a service contract.

In the scenario of Universal Revenue Recognition, a new period-end close application job has been introduced, Run Revenue Recognition – Revenue Contracts, that performs the period end close for revenue contracts and its underlying operational documents, such as sales order, service contract and customer projects (Step 4). As a result, the usual period end close programs you may know from revenue accounting are not required anymore.

All posting are directly posting into the Universal Journal, with revenue contract and performance obligation as attributes in the posting lines (Step 5).

If you're interested, stay tuned for further detailed information on the individual scenarios and how universal revenue recognition will create this ‘one-stop shopping’ revenue recognition experience. This is just the beginning and URR capabilities will be growing steadily over time.

Additional Information from our user assistance you can find here:

Event-Based Revenue Recognition: SAP Help EBRR

Contract-Based Revenue Recognition: SAP Help CBRR

Universal Revenue Recognition:   SAP Help URR

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign - State Wise GST Configuration
entry.content:
Dear Vijay Kannan,

You have to create the separate supplier/customer master for each GST registration number of the single supplier/customer, with different supplying and billing addresses as per need and business requirement.

Best Regards,

Prathibha

entry.title: Re: SAP Build App data connection with Odata Integration.
entry.content:
As I always do with a bug, I try to check every part of he connection. That is difficult with the table, so first I do it with data variable.

  • Can you see data from your data resource in the data table?
  • If you create a data variable and connect to text component, can you see data?
  • If all that is fine, back on the table, can you check the configuraion that you are outputting fields that are not null?
  • Are you returning an enormous  data set that it is too big for SAP Build Apps? You can use the pagination features inside the table.

It is tough to diagnose the issue without knowing more and doing tests along the way.

All in all, my personal opinion is that the table component is not the best to use, and it is better o make your own table component using a data variable.

entry.title: Re: CustomData attribute (data-) not working as documente...
entry.content: I'm happy to file a formal request or even to try to implement this. Do you know where I can suggest/open suggestion issues for UI5 in an effective way?

entry.title: SOURCING & PROCUREMENT - PURCHASE RATE CONTRACT IN SAP CLOUD
entry.content:
Dear Team

Basically our clients requirement is RATE CONTRACT but in SAP we have only quantity contract & value contract. We want when We  create po with reference to  value contract , price & condition should copy in po from value contract and it should not be editable in PO. user will only enter quantity in po.  Also we are not able to add condition in value contract like basic price , discount etc. Please let us know whether schema can assign to Contract or we have to add condition through condition record.   

 

Rajesh

entry.title: The Love-Hate Relationship with Networking
entry.content:
Networking. Probably one of the most polarizing soft skills in the business world. A real love-hate relationship you could say. It comes natural to some and can be energizing, while others just wish they would never have to do it because it drains their social batteries and increases their anxiety. Many swear by it and encourage to use every opportunity we get to grow our network. But do we really need it to have a successful career?

To me the question here is more one about perspective. But let me share some of my insights on what I learned about networking from our recent Summer Summit collaboration between the Business Women’s Network and SAP Women in Tech. A few weeks ago, we organized our first ever joined Summer Summit on-site in Walldorf. A little bit late for a summer summit in September, some might say, but we had the sun gracing us with one of those last warm days this year. We were lucky enough to have Traci Hughes present us with a short keynote on the topic of (you probably guessed it): Networking. And while at first people were expecting some encouraging words to please start networking because it is career essential, Traci managed to find a slightly more nuanced perspective on the topic.

Let me sum up the most important things I learned from that evening. Being an outgoing person myself, I am of course not as aware of all the aspects. But even I have encountered events and situations where I just could not bring myself to talk to another stranger, or a semi-acquaintance and discuss the current weather conditions because it felt incredibly draining.

The bad news first: networking really can be helpful in your professional development.

However, it might be a relief for some that when it comes to networking, quality can weigh a lot more than quantity. Which means fostering or working on good connections can be more helpful than just having plenty without really knowing someone.

Something I also learned about networking is that it kind of works like a garden. Meaning it grows over time and harvesting the fruits of your networking can take quite a while. Personally, I still feel like I am waiting for that bountiful harvest 😉 but you never know, right?

Lastly, I think it is important to control that pressure we are putting on ourselves to constantly perform our best social skills or come up with the most entertaining anecdote and topic to talk to someone. In a nutshell: being someone we are not. And if you benefit from good quality networks that are none superficial, doesn’t trying to be someone else counterfeit that purpose? Meaning, be yourself. And if that means you won’t talk to 10 new people at every given opportunity, I think that’s absolutely justified behavior.

Because the really good conversations that help you in your professional development are those that feel authentically you. And let me tell you: those conversations, we have had plenty of at our Summer Event. 😊

At long last I want to encourage all readers to look for those authentic conversations that will grow your network. And yes, you might have to nudge yourself a little bit outside of your comfort zone at times. Yes, there will certainly be those awkward moments around new people, but I don’t think anyone has ever grown without being a little uncomfortable throughout the process. Do you?

P.S. Feel free to share some of your awkward networking stories with the community!!

entry.title: Re: Please explain Conditional router in detail in CPI
entry.content:
Hi Sriprasad,

 

 

I am using looping process call with condition.

${property.value1} != 'one' or ${property.value2} != 'two'. 

this is not working for me when i am giving two non xml property with or operation

in locall intergration process i am setting content modifier with value1 as two and value2 as two. 

so second time it should not come to local intergration process. but it is iterating again.

 

Please let me know if the syntax is incorrect and how can i correct it. It will be a great help

 

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Remove and add Measure in a button
entry.content: Hi, thanks for the answer. I did the exercise and adapted it to my code, but it doesn’t seem right. The measures don’t show up. The only difference is that I have measures that I imported from an Excel file, and I don’t have an account dimension. Does that make a difference?

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi @tskwin ,

I checked my settings im IAS and they are as follows:

Application Settings of the BTP subaccount:

  • Subject name identifier: Identity Directory, Email
  • Attributes: email, email_verified, family_name, given_name, user_uuid -> all sourced from Identity Directory, only groups comes from the corporate IDP as described in the blog

Corporate Identity Provider:

  • Subject name identifier: None (sub claim is the identifier)

I created a fresh BTP trial account and IAS trial tenant for this tutorial and did not change any of the default settings for the subject name identifiers and attributes. Only the groups attribute is added as described.

You may also want to go this path, or  change you settings accordingly. Let us know if it works.

Best regards

Martin

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: I don't have pets, but my girlfriend brought over an observer for me to pair-program with. 🐻

entry.title: Re: Volume /hana/log is full with log segments in state F...
entry.content:
You can safely release log segments anytime using the SQL statement below. There is no need to stop the application. (You must not use OS commands, like rm, to remove log segments. This would make your instance unusable.). 
The SQL will just remove log segments in status FREE, which are neither required for crash recovery nor any other purpose (HSR, to be backed up, etc.). 
The only drawback is, that HANA has to format new log segments next time there are no log segments in status FREE. Usually, formatting log segments is not an issue unless you suffer from slow disk I/O.
On the other hand there is no need to remove any log segments unless you want to free the disk space. Keep in mind that XFS file system does not support reducing the file system size at all, even not offline (just in case you use XFS). 
Regards, 
  Alexander
